negativePrompts.note: Flux 2 does not support negative prompts. Use semantic negatives in the main prompt instead, e.g. 'without text' rather than a negative prompt.

strengths: Strong prompt adherence for complex scenes
tips: Use detailed, natural-language scene descriptions rather than keyword lists
Specify lighting, camera angle, and atmosphere explicitly
Include art style references when aiming for non-photorealistic output
Describe spatial relationships between subjects clearly
Mention material textures and surface qualities for realism
avoid: Keyword-style tag lists separated by commas
Negative prompt syntax or embedded negatives
Overly short prompts with minimal detail

tips: Start with the main subject, then layer in environment and mood
Specify aspect ratio intent in the description if composition matters
Use cinematic or photographic framing terms for dynamic compositions
Describe the desired changes relative to the source image
Focus on what should change rather than re-describing the entire scene
Mention style transfer goals explicitly if changing artistic direction
Be specific and descriptive in your prompt
Include details about subject, setting, lighting, and style
Use clear, unambiguous language to describe the desired output
avoid: Contradictory instructions in the same prompt
